Preah Khan was far more than just a temple; it was a bustling city, a major Buddhist university, and a symbol of a  ...    ### Victory and Dedication Constructed in 1191 AD, Preah Khan holds deep historical significance. It was built by King Jayavarman VII on the very site where he won a decisive victory against the invading Cham forces. The temple was dedicated to his father, Dharanindravarman II, who was posthumously represented as the bodhisattva of compassion, Lokesvara (Avalokiteshvara).
